title: Per-language translations of reviewed content
context: Non-English speakers are disproportionately among those who need access-to-justice resources and have the fewest alternatives. Know-Your-Rights will offer reviewed translations of its attorney-approved content, but machine-translating legal claims is dangerous — errors in translation can mislead people in high-stakes situations. Each language requires a combined legal review (a licensed attorney who reads the language and can verify accuracy in the named jurisdiction) and a native-speaker linguistic review before any translated legal claim ships. This task covers all per-language translations and is backlog: not started until M3 i18n scaffolding (task 014) is complete.
objective: Produce reviewed translations of the pilot tenant-rights content (tasks 009-010) into at least one non-English language, driven by partner need. Each translation requires: a native-speaker translator; a licensed attorney admitted in the jurisdiction who can read the target language to verify legal accuracy; and an independent native-speaker linguistic reviewer. No machine-translated legal claims may ship. Each translated language is a separate, attorney-gated deliverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Translations produced for at least one non-English language confirmed as a partner need (e.g., Spanish, Simplified Chinese, or another language determined by partner demographics)

Each translated explainer and wallet card is produced by a human translator — no machine-translated legal claims shipped; machine translation may be used only as a drafting aid, never as the final text
A licensed attorney admitted in the pilot jurisdiction who reads the target language verifies the legal accuracy of each translated claim before delivery; sign-off recorded and version-scoped

An independent native-speaker linguistic reviewer (separate from the translator) reviews the translation for fluency, plain-language clarity, and reading-level compliance (explainers <= grade 8, cards <= grade 6 as measured in the target language); review recorded
Translated content carries the 'not legal advice' banner and route-to-help block translated into the target language
All translated content passes through the i18n review-gate build markers from task 014 (.reviewed-legal + .reviewed-linguistic) before being included in the build output
Translated content is schema-valid per task 003 with a language field added to the record
Sequenced after task 014 (i18n scaffolding); each language is a separate gated deliverable
output: Reviewed translations of the tenant-rights explainer set and wallet card into at least one confirmed partner-need language — human-translated, attorney-verified for legal accuracy in the jurisdiction, native-speaker linguistically reviewed, lint-clean, and carrying the 'not legal advice' banner in the target language.
